marketFocus: Operation Epic Fury Day 7: Iran-Krieg eskaliert — Strait of Hormuz near-total halt, WTI settled $81.01/bbl (+$6.35), Brent $85.41/bbl (+$4.01); US CENTCOM: ballistic missile attacks by Iran down 90% since Day 1, but Hegseth says 'firepower to increase significantly'; Iran FM refuses negotiations; EU Stoxx -1.5%, SPX -0.56%, DXY peaked 99.41 before trimming to 99.05 on China-Iran Hormuz talks

NFP Day (14:30 Frankfurt): Feb NFP exp. 59K (vs 130K Jan); Unemployment Claims Thu 213K (in-line, 215K exp); Unit Labour Costs Q4 +2.8% (hot, vs +2.0% exp); Import Prices +0.2% MoM (in-line); Challenger Layoffs Feb 48K (down from 108K Jan) — labour market resilient but war uncertainty dominates; Retail Sales Jan also due 14:30

AUD/NZD under pressure: China 2026 GDP target 4.5%-5.0% (lowest since 1991), base metals lower, gold hit by NBP gold-sales-for-defence report (gold fell $5,160→$5,092 in 5 mins); GBP outperformed EUR as asset managers unwind long EUR positions; CHF outperformed JPY as premier haven (SNB sight deposits CHF 457.6bln)

USD.summary: DXY maintained its haven bid with a range of 98.66-99.41 on Thursday. Risk-off sentiment from the Iran war, WTI surging to $81/bbl, and better-than-expected US data (Jobless Claims 213K vs 215K exp, Unit Labour Costs +2.8% hot) all support USD. China-Iran Hormuz talks briefly trimmed USD strength late session. Today is NFP day — Feb exp. 59K, Unemployment Rate exp. 4.0%, Retail Sales exp. +0.2%. Fed speakers Daly, Collins, Hammack all due today. War premium + NFP uncertainty = USD supported on dips.
drivers: DXY 98.66-99.41 — haven bid intact, Iran war premium
Jobless Claims 213K (vs 215K exp, beat) — labour market resilient
Unit Labour Costs Q4 +2.8% (vs +2.0% exp, hot) — inflationary signal
NFP Feb exp. 59K + Retail Sales Jan due 14:30 Frankfurt — key catalyst
Fed Daly, Collins, Hammack speak today — war impact assessment

EUR.summary: EUR/USD traded 1.1581-1.1647 on Thursday, pressured by energy-import concerns and ECB stagflation risk. ECB Minutes noted 'several argued rates could remain at current levels for extended period'. ECB Lagarde: meeting-by-meeting, no pre-set stance. ECB de Guindos: could change policy if inflation expectations change due to war. EU Retail Sales Jan -0.1% MoM (vs +0.3% exp, miss). EUR/USD returned to 1.1600 handle Friday morning but upside limited. Morgan Stanley expects ECB to hold all 2026. ECB Lagarde, Cipollone, Schnabel all speak today.
drivers: EUR/USD 1.1581-1.1647 — pressured by energy shock, fragile above 1.1600
ECB Minutes: several see rates on hold for extended period
ECB de Guindos: policy could change if inflation expectations shift
EU Retail Sales Jan -0.1% MoM (vs +0.3% exp, miss)
ECB Lagarde, Cipollone, Schnabel speak today — key guidance

GBP.summary: GBP/USD traded 1.3298-1.3387, rebounding from 1.3298 support. ING noted GBP outperformance vs EUR as asset managers unwind long EUR positions and markets push back on near-term BoE cuts. GBP/USD lacked direction Friday morning — price action tame with quiet UK newsflow. UK 10yr yield above 4.5%. BoE cut path intact (Goldman Sachs: April cut). UK is a net energy importer — Iran war = stagflationary headwind. No UK domestic data today. GBP structurally bearish but relatively better than EUR in the current environment.
drivers: GBP/USD 1.3298-1.3387 — rebounded from 1.3298 support
ING: GBP outperforms EUR as asset managers unwind EUR longs
UK 10yr yield above 4.5% — Gilt market under pressure
BoE cut path intact: Goldman Sachs April cut, then July, November
No UK data today — geopolitics and Iran narrative drive price action

JPY.summary: USD/JPY traded 156.44-157.78 on Thursday. JPY benefits from haven demand as Iran war escalates, with carry trade liquidation supporting the Yen. BoJ Deputy Governor Himino confirmed Japan is seeing inflation in rising consumer prices and BoJ is gradually adjusting monetary accommodation. Bloomberg officials see little chance of March hike but do not rule out April. USD/JPY edged higher Friday morning as risk sentiment in Japan stabilised and APAC stocks clawed back losses. JPY remains the second-best haven after CHF in the current environment.
drivers: USD/JPY 156.44-157.78 — haven bid, carry liquidation support
BoJ Himino: Japan seeing inflation, gradually adjusting accommodation
Bloomberg: March hike unlikely, April not ruled out
USD/JPY edged higher Friday as APAC risk stabilised
JPY second-best haven after CHF — Iran war premium intact

AUD.summary: AUD/USD hit as low as 0.6974 on Thursday as geopolitical risks, weaker metal prices, and China's cut to 2026 GDP growth target (4.5%-5.0%, lowest since 1991) weighed heavily. ASX 200 dragged lower by miners, materials and resources sectors. Gold hit by NBP gold-sales-for-defence report. Antipodeans strengthened mildly Friday morning with AUD outperforming as metal prices rebounded and outside bets for an RBA rate hike this month emerged. However, the recovery is fragile — Iran war escalation remains the dominant tail risk. RBA Hauser speaks today.
drivers: AUD/USD hit 0.6974 low — geopolitics, weak metals, China GDP cut
China 2026 GDP target 4.5%-5.0% (lowest since 1991) — structural headwind
ASX 200 lower: miners, materials, resources dragged by metal weakness
Gold hit by NBP gold-sales-for-defence report — AUD felt the blow
RBA Hauser speaks today — watch for March rate hike guidance

CAD.summary: CAD caught between energy windfall (WTI $81/bbl = direct terms-of-trade positive) and domestic weakness (Q4 GDP -0.6% annualized). Canada-US Trade Minister LeBlanc visiting Washington today. Canada-EU signed deal to modernise trade agreement. BoC Macklem spoke Wednesday — no major new guidance. CAD Employment and Unemployment Rate due today at 14:30 Frankfurt alongside US NFP. Weekly Bias Neutral — no High Conviction Setup alignment possible. CAD remains a cross-current currency: energy positive, growth negative.
drivers: WTI $81/bbl — energy windfall positive for CAD terms-of-trade
Q4 GDP -0.6% annualized (weakest outside pandemic years) — growth drag
Canada-EU trade deal modernisation signed
CAD Employment + Unemployment Rate due 14:30 Frankfurt today
Weekly Bias Neutral — no High Conviction alignment

CHF.summary: CHF is the premier haven in G10, outperforming even JPY. SNB sight deposits rose to CHF 457.6bln (intervention monitoring). Morgan Stanley projects EUR/CHF down to 0.87. CHF rallied vs most G10 peers during risk-off Thursday, but remains unattractive on carry in stable risk environments. Switzerland's low inflation, energy independence, and SNB's cautious stance make CHF the cleanest safe haven. SNB March 19 meeting is the key near-term event — hot Swiss CPI Feb (+0.6% MoM) reduces probability of a cut. Swiss Unemployment Rate 3.2% (stable).
drivers: Premier haven: outperformed JPY, SNB sight deposits CHF 457.6bln
Morgan Stanley: EUR/CHF target 0.87 — structural CHF strength
Swiss CPI Feb +0.6% MoM (hot) — reduces SNB March 19 cut probability
Swiss Unemployment Rate 3.2% (stable, in-line)
CHF rallied vs most G10 during Thursday risk-off session

NZD.summary: NZD is the most vulnerable G10 currency in the current risk-off environment. The Kiwi underperformed even AUD as carry unwinds and trade-related growth fears intensified. RBNZ dovish pivot under Governor Breman creates a domestic rates handicap reinforced by global risk-off. NZD is typically the first currency cut in risk-off episodes. Antipodeans strengthened mildly Friday morning alongside AUD. No NZD domestic data today — entirely driven by China headlines and global risk sentiment. Weekly Strong Bearish + Daily Bearish = perfect alignment for shorts.
drivers: Most vulnerable G10 — first cut in risk-off, carry unwind target
RBNZ dovish pivot: OCR at current levels near term
China 2026 GDP target 4.5%-5.0% (lowest since 1991) — NZD negative
NZD mildly firmer Friday morning — fragile recovery alongside AUD
No domestic data today — entirely sentiment and China driven

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: T-note futures settled 16 ticks lower at 112-10 on Thursday. 10yr yield at 4.125% (+4.3bps), 2yr at 3.574% (+3.1bps). Energy prices continue to rise, stoking inflationary fears. European nat gas prices rising — ECB rate hike probability by year-end now 75% (vs 25% Tuesday). Fed likely to hold in March/April. Rabo: under Warsh, June cut possible if Fed looks through energy price effects. NFP today is the key catalyst for yield direction.
drivers: T-notes settled 16 ticks lower at 112-10 — energy inflation fears
ECB rate hike probability by year-end: 75% (vs 25% Tuesday)
Rabo: Warsh may push for June cut, looking through energy effects
NFP today 14:30 Frankfurt — key yield direction catalyst

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bunds ended Thursday off by ~90 ticks, at the bottom end of 127.29-128.03 range. ECB de Guindos: could change policy if inflation expectations change. ECB Minutes: several see rates on hold for extended period. German pensions to increase 4.24% from July — mild inflationary pressure. Bund futures attempting to nurse losses Friday morning but recovery contained ahead of NFP. EZ GDP 3rd estimate Q4 due today.
drivers: Bunds off ~90 ticks — 127.29-128.03 range, bottom end
ECB de Guindos: policy could change if inflation expectations shift
ECB Minutes: several see rates on hold for extended period
German pensions +4.24% from July — mild inflationary signal
EZ GDP 3rd estimate Q4 due today — Bund direction catalyst

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: JGB futures choppy Thursday amid tentative mood in Japan with lack of tier-1 data. 10yr JGB futures languished near prior day's lows. BoJ Himino confirmed gradual adjustment of monetary accommodation. April hike not ruled out. JGB futures choppy Friday morning amid indecisive APAC mood.
drivers: JGB futures choppy — tentative mood, no tier-1 data
BoJ Himino: gradually adjusting monetary accommodation
April hike not ruled out per Bloomberg sources
JGB futures choppy Friday morning — indecisive APAC mood
USD/JPY 156.44-157.78 — approaching intervention zone

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts ended Thursday lower by ~100 ticks, within 91.18-92.15 range. UK 10yr yield above 4.5%. Underperformance a factor of catch-up to prior day's action across peers. PM Starmer press conference on Iran offered no impulses. Goldman Sachs: BoE April cut. No UK data today — Gilt direction driven by global risk sentiment.
drivers: Gilts off ~100 ticks — 91.18-92.15 range, underperformance vs peers
UK 10yr yield above 4.5% — elevated
PM Starmer: Iran conflict could continue for some time
Goldman Sachs: BoE April cut, then July, November
No UK data today — global risk sentiment and Iran narrative
